title: Draft 2020-12 and semantic validation evidence is unavailable
problem: The package relies on a pinned Gherkin parser, Draft 2020-12 keywords, cross-file references, and semantic invariants, but the reports record parser/standards validation as unavailable and `$ref`/semantic validation as PENDING.
evidence: schema-validation-report.md reports JSON parse PASS but Draft 2020-12 capability NOT RUN and `$ref` resolution/semantic fixtures PENDING; the bounded Gherkin check could not run the required parser. The matrix lists causal ordering, approval consumption, replay side-effect prohibition, budget conservation, and Task Manager ownership as semanticOnly.
impact: A JSON parse pass cannot establish enforceable completion, approval, replay, or state-transition contracts. Completion policy blocks readiness on this gap.
suggested_fix: Add pinned Gherkin and Draft 2020-12 validators plus a deterministic semantic runner; persist parser/validator versions, resolved refs, fixture hashes, and failures. Keep the package draft until they run.
verification: Run the parser and validate all schemas/catalogs, then execute terminal-state, causal-reference, approval, replay, budget, and ownership checks.

title: Harness orchestration ownership remains ambiguous
line_or_section: Planned Module Map; Orchestration Model; Canonical Ownership
problem: D2 makes Task Manager the sole managed-flow owner, but the module map still reserves `src/harness/orchestration/` for plan/execute/review/fix coordination and the lifecycle describes follow-up-loop behavior. No hard import/authority boundary separates non-managed turns.
evidence: The ownership table forbids a second managed loop, while the module map names an orchestration module and the orchestration section lists plan/execute/verify, parallel wave, and review/fix modes. No forbidden-call rule is machine-checkable.
impact: Implementers can create a second scheduler or completion authority, recreating S-06 despite D7.
suggested_fix: Rename the harness module to execution primitives/turn control, state managed orchestration is an external adapter only, and add a machine-checkable import/ownership matrix with lease semantics.
verification: Architecture review confirms one coordinator, one completion evaluator, and no harness write path to managed-flow state.

title: Deprecated child-task schema remains in normative fixture coverage
problem: `harness-agent-task.schema.json` is deprecated, but it still has positive and negative fixture entries and is listed with active schema families. No concrete gate proves new transports cannot accept it.
evidence: The schema has `deprecated: true`, while fixture-matrix.json maps both positive and negative cases and README lists it as a package schema. The canonical extension is separate, but no validator rule rejects deprecated-task persistence.
impact: Workers can continue treating the deprecated shape as a valid child contract, preserving S-08's second source of truth.
suggested_fix: Move it to a migration-only catalog, prohibit it in transport/persistence validation, and add a negative fixture for new dispatch; retain only adapter migration tests.
verification: Contract review confirms canonical subagent-dispatch/result parity and a failing negative test for deprecated-task use.

title: Schema compatibility policy is not machine-checkable
problem: The inventory promises additive minor versions, incompatible major versions, compatibility ranges, and persistence of original versions, while schemas only constrain integer `schemaVersion` to const 1 and no compatibility registry maps accepted ranges or migration functions.
evidence: contract-inventory.md requires readers to accept declared compatibility ranges and write the newest version; the schema set has no declared range or registry entry beyond `schemaVersion: 1`, and migration fixtures are absent.
impact: A future schema change can silently break replay/session opening or rewrite history without a deterministic compatibility decision.
suggested_fix: Add a version registry with schema id, stored version, accepted range, migration id, and rejection behavior; align schema fields or scope semver compatibility to an implementation artifact.
verification: Validate one old-version migration and one incompatible-version rejection for every durable contract family.
